title: lactuca virosa subsp. cornigera
intro: Lactuca virosa subsp. cornigera, also known as wild lettuce or prickly lettuce, is a subspecies of the Lactuca virosa plant, which is a biennial or perennial plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its sedative and analgesic properties, as it contains lactucarium, a glycoside with medicinal properties. The health benefits of Lactuca virosa subsp. cornigera include its ability to reduce anxiety, promote relaxation, and alleviate pain, making it a popular herbal remedy for insomnia and other sleep disorders. Herbal preparations of Lactuca virosa subsp. cornigera can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
